articles.title: Mystery Explosion in Space: Could It Be a 'Superkilonova'?
articles.summary: Astronomers may have spotted a brand-new type of cosmic explosion — a 'superkilonova.' The event, AT2025ulz, started like a kilonova (a neutron star collision that makes gold) but then turned into a supernova. Scientists think a supernova might have triggered a hidden neutron star crash inside it. If confirmed, it would be the first observation of this long-predicted phenomenon.

articles.title: The Search for LUCA: Our Billion-Year-Old Grandparent
articles.summary: Scientists are piecing together the story of LUCA, the Last Universal Common Ancestor of all life on Earth. LUCA was a single-celled organism that lived about 4 billion years ago, probably near a deep-sea hydrothermal vent. Every living thing today — from humans to bacteria to mushrooms — is a descendant of LUCA. New research has revealed more details about when LUCA lived and how it survived.

articles.title: A Roboticist-Turned-Teacher and His Students Built a Life-Size ENIAC Replica
articles.summary: Tom Burick, a technology teacher at a school for neurodivergent students, led his class in building a full-scale replica of ENIAC, one of the world's first electronic computers. Burick, who has dyscalculia, previously ran a robotics company. He says his learning differences gave him unique strengths, like 3D spatial reasoning, which he now uses to inspire his students.
